Apache Camel is a comprehensive integration framework spanning 48 audited files across core engine, DSL layers, and 300+ components. The architecture exhibits clean separation of concerns: core engine (AbstractCamelContext, BaseMainSupport) manages lifecycle and service discovery; model layer (ProcessorDefinition) defines route structures with visitor patterns for transformation; I/O layers generate Java/XML/YAML serialization code; and domain components (Kafka, JMS, File, Keycloak) implement endpoint/producer/consumer patterns. Generated code (JavaDslModelWriter, ModelWriter, ConverterLoaders) reflects metaprogramming tooling, while extensive test suites ensure language evaluation (SimpleTest) and integration correctness. HL7 healthcare converters dominate the file count, demonstrating deep specialization. The framework balances configuration flexibility (300+ properties across JMS, Kafka, Debezium components) with type safety through annotation-driven binding and JAXB marshaling.
